description: Tissue-specific targeting and delivery optimization for genetic medicines - ligands, formulations, and biodistribution.

facts.context: GalNAc (N-acetylgalactosamine) conjugation enables hepatocyte targeting by:
distractors: crossing hepatocyte membranes passively
sequestering albumin in portal vein
activating Kupffer cell phagocytosis

facts.context: Transferrin receptor targeting for brain delivery works because:
facts.truth: TfR on brain endothelium mediates transcytosis
distractors: transferrin permeates lipid bilayers directly
TfR expression concentrates in peripheral tissue
iron chelation disrupts tight junction integrity

facts.context: Antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) achieve tumor selectivity through:
facts.truth: antigen binding, internalization, then payload release
distractors: uniform biodistribution across all tissue types
extracellular payload cleavage in tumor stroma
complement-mediated lysis at the tumor surface

facts.context: LNPs naturally accumulate in liver because:
facts.truth: ApoE adsorption targets hepatocyte LDL receptors
distractors: hepatic sinusoids filter by particle charge
Kupffer cells selectively endocytose lipid cores
bile salt coating drives hepatocyte uptake

facts.context: LNP delivery to spleen and immune cells can be enhanced by:
facts.truth: incorporating anionic lipids or adjusting PEG ratios
distractors: using standard hepatocyte-targeting formulations
increasing particle diameter beyond 500 nanometers
coating particles with cationic polymer shells

facts.context: AAV serotypes with muscle tropism (AAV8, AAV9) achieve this by:
facts.truth: binding specific surface receptors on muscle fibers
distractors: transducing all tissue types at equal efficiency
accumulating via higher viral titer concentration
exploiting nonspecific electrostatic membrane binding

facts.context: Intrathecal delivery of gene therapies for CNS diseases:
facts.truth: bypasses BBB, reaching CSF and spinal cord
distractors: distributes identically to intravenous injection
restricts expression to brain cortex regions
requires systemic doses to reach motor neurons

facts.context: Subretinal injection for retinal gene therapy:
facts.truth: accesses RPE and photoreceptors with minimal systemic exposure
distractors: distributes evenly across all ocular compartments
requires concurrent systemic immunosuppressive therapy
transduces ganglion cells but not photoreceptors
particles of all sizes distribute identically
larger particles penetrate more tissue barriers
renal clearance dominates above 200 nanometers

facts.context: Endosomal escape is critical for nucleic acid delivery because:
facts.truth: cargo must reach cytoplasm or nucleus to function
distractors: endosomal compartments serve as activation sites
lysosomal degradation enhances therapeutic effect
endosomal pH triggers spontaneous cargo release
